text: Ɔluakɛ ɛnee wɔ nyɛmoa le ekyi bie yɛɛ membale a, kɛkala bɛ nwo ɛzo dɔɔnwo kpalɛ, yɛɛ medame me ti, Ɛdɛnkɛma ɛyila wɔ wɔ debie biala anu. Na kɛkala menea a akee yedwu mekɛ mɔɔ ɔwɔ kɛ medwenle me sua nu amra anwo a.”
translated: The elders and the young men in the town have all agreed that we should go to the outskirts of the town to clear the weeds, so I am informing you, Edinkema has said we should meet at the base of the big tree. And when we finish, we will come back home to eat and rest a little.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na mɔɔ nrenya ne nwunle kɛ yeangola yeangomo Gyekɔbo anwo zo la, ɔvale ɔ sa ɔhanle Gyekɔbo ɛzɔlɛ, na Gyekɔbo adwongu huanenle wɔ ɛfutulɛ ne anu.
translated: And the elders and the youth of the town said that Gyekɔbo's mother was a witch, she was selling her witchcraft to Gyekɔbo, and Gyekɔbo's bad deeds were many in the town and the bush.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Yemɔti Nyamenle bɔle sonla kɛ ɔdaye ye ɛsaso la; ɔdaye Nyamenle mumua ne ye ɛsaso a ɔnleanle ɔbɔle sonla a. Ɔbɔle bɛ nrenya nee raalɛ.
translated: Nyamenle's child was born and he named him ɛsaso; Nyamenle's child grew up and ɛsaso became a strong person. He became a hunter and a warrior.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Yemɔti fa gɔfa baka bobɔ ɛlɛnemgbole; kyɛ nuhua ekpumgbunli ekpumgbunli na fa bilewu ka nuhua nee nwolɛ amuala.
translated: The hunter's dog has caught a big animal in the forest; but the hunter has not come to the house to tell his wife and children the good news.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Na mebava azɛlɛ ne mɔɔ ɛlɛkpɔsakpɔsa zolɛ la meamaa wɔ nee ɛ bo zo amra. Bɛbava Keena azɛlɛ ne kɔsɔɔti bɛayɛ bɛ ɛdeɛ, na mebayɛ bɛ Nyamenle.”
